rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF BERTA CONSUELO LOARCA-HERRERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/03/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Issues: 1) Conservator of the Person, Barbara deVries, is requesting 1% fees of $5,313.82 based on the local rules. The timesheets provided allege $7,126.75 in services. However, the timesheets provided show services done by S. Goldberg with no explanation for their qualifications or legal basis for why they should be paid the same rate as the conservator of the estate. Unclear what special skills are needed to pick up mail, review, or attend to mail, write checks. 9+ hours to work on schedules of a 6th account appears unusually high. Please explain. 2) The proposed order does not match the relief requested in the second amendment. Please provide an updated proposed order to the Examiner in room 202. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LINDA YEE LIN MA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/03/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please prove the estate is entirely community property of Decedent. 2) Decedent died 08/29/2016. Unclear when account begins as page 4 line 2-3 alleges the account begins 08/28/2016 the summary of account alleges the beginning assets on hand on 08/28/2016 and Schedule A alleges beginning assets on hand 08/29/2016. A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. (See LRSF 14.34). 3) Petition must be signed and verified by Petitioner. All pleadings filed in proceedings under the Probate Code must be verified. If two or more persons join in a pleading, it may be verified by any of them. (See CRC 7.103). 4) The petition alleges the I&A value of $601,834.77 contains all the assets of Decedent's ancillary probate estate. Please amend the account to begin with the I&A value or file a memorandum of points and authorities in support of the account beginning with $1,203,669.54. 5) Please explain and file receipts for all $7,612.38 in alleged owed expenses to Executor. Please address Probate Code 9252. 6) Local Rule 14.57 B says mileage and parking are absorbed as part of fees. It appears the "travel fees" on disbursements totaling $1,747.25 must be paid back to the estate and a receipt filed. 7) Please explain and file a receipt for 01/18/2018 Tenant Search Fees Adam Savick for $2,300.00. 8) The petition alleges the account period ends 08/28/2018. The 11/04/2021 Declaration has a bank statement from April 22, 2016 to May 20, 2016. Please verify the balance at the end of the account period. See LRSF 14.34 D. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MATTHEW SHUI SANG MA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/03/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please prove the estate is entirely community property of Decedent. 2) Decedent died 08/29/2016. Unclear when account begins as page 1 line 21 alleges the account begins 08/28/2016, page 4 line 2 alleges the account begins 08/28/2018, the summary of account alleges the beginning assets on hand on 08/28/2016 and Schedule A alleges beginning assets on hand 08/29/2016. A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. (See LRSF 14.34). 3) Petition must be signed and verified by Petitioner. All pleadings filed in proceedings under the Probate Code must be verified. If two or more persons join in a pleading, it may be verified by any of them. (See CRC 7.103). 4) The petition alleges the I&A value of $601,834.77 contains all the assets of Decedent's ancillary probate estate. Please amend the account to begin with the I&A value or file a memorandum of points and authorities in support of the account beginning with $1,203,669.54. 5) Please explain and file receipts for all $8,477.38 in alleged owed expenses to Executor. Please address Probate Code 9252. 6) Please explain on schedule D - Disbursements Appraisal of 300 Berry St., Unit 1108 for $350.00 and file a receipt. Please address Probate Code 8900 et seq and why this appraisal was necessary when the court appointed a probate referee. 7) Local Rule 14.57 B says mileage and parking are absorbed as part of fees. It appears the "travel fees" on disbursements totaling $1,747.25 must be paid back to the estate and a receipt filed. 8) Please explain and file a receipt for 01/18/2018 Tenant Search Fees Adam Savick for $2,300.00. 9) The petition alleges the account period ends 08/28/2018. The 11/04/2021 Declaration has a bank statement from April 22, 2016 to May 20, 2016. Please verify the balance at the end of the account period. See LRSF 14.34 D. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOAN RUTH LANE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/17/22. No earlier availability. Notice was not given to Mae Lazarus or Jeanne Matthew. (It is unclear why notice was given to Claudia Ayala and Pilar Ayala, who are not beneficiaries.) From 6/22/18 to 4/5/22, there were 1,382 days. 1,382 days x .19 = $262.58. $1,500 x 7% divided by 365 = $.29/day x 1,382 days = $400.78. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCES JOSEPHINE THOMPSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition To Determine Heirship And Entitlement To Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/18/22. No earlier available date. Decedent's death certificate (attachment 11) does not identify either parent. Is there a birth certificate for her or anything proving who her parents were? There are no supporting documents for first cousin Theodore Ritchie and first cousin once removed Elicia Morrow Elliott. Was John Michael Ritchie not also survived by issue? Even if they purportedly died without issue, there should be supporting documents for uncles (aunt?) John Lincoln Ritchie and William Anthony Ritchie and Glennon Ritchie and for first cousin once removed Edmund Ritchie. Exhibit F also does not have any supporting documents for any of the paternal aunt and uncles (Patricia Louise Ritchie Ripley's marriage certificate identifies her father as Theodore JEROME Ritchie, not Theodore James Arnold Ritchie) The addresses of the heirs are not set forth. Notice does not appear to have been given to Theodore Ritchie or Ann L. Ritchie. Paragraph 27 of the petition shows the sister of Jennifer Christine Ritchie Martin as Deborah Lee Ritchie Ripley, but she is shown on the heirship chart as Deborah Lee Ritchie Penikas. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/9/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AURELIA A. WOODARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/19/2022 by judicial officer. Petitioner needs to prove by admissible evidence that either the presumption doesn't apply or the presumption needs to be rebutted. Demonstrative by admissible evidence the testator didn't have original will at time of her death and that testator lacked capacity at the time of her death. The other lettered paragraphs in the attachment do not address the applicability or rebuttal of probate code 6124.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HIROYUKI KAJIMA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition To Use Spouse'S Sperm Post-Mortem
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/24/2022. This tentative ruling is issued by the judicial officer. Petitioner was asked to file a memorandum of points and authorities regarding jurisdiction and venue. The memorandum fails to address convincingly these issues. None of the presented probate provisions authorize a petition of this kind in the probate court and none of the authorities support the requested relief in the context of this petition. If these requests are properly presented to a probate court, Petitioner needs to present them in an estate proceeding for Hiroyuki Kajima after appointment of an estate representative or explain why she need not proceed this way. At the proper time (likely after appointment of an estate representative), Petitioner must file a second amended petition that conforms to requirements of the Probate Code, including proper allegations regarding: (1) statutory basis for petition; (2) jurisdiction; (3) venue; and, (4) factual and legal basis for all relief requested. The second amended petition must contain allegations overcoming the presumption that Hiroyuki Kajima did not intend his gametic material to be used for posthumous conception and making an affirmative showing that Hiroyuki Kajima intended: (1) Petitioner to have "full ownership" over his gametic material after his death (recognizing that gametic material is a unique type of property not governed by general laws regarding gifts or transfers upon death); (2) Petitioner to control the disposition of his gametic material upon his death; and, (3) intended to allow the use of his gametic material for posthumous conception." (See Robertson v. Saadat (2020) 48 Cal.App.5th 630, 640-648.) If Hiroyuki Kajima's intent is not evidence by a writing or writings, the second amended petition should address why a writing should not be required, as it is in the heirship context under Probate Code section 249.5. (See id. at p. 647 [reserving decision whether intent must be evidenced by writing].) Amended petition must include a copy of the sperm storage agreement, as well as copies of the executed UCSF forms referenced in the amended petition. The second amended petition must be properly noticed and the papers properly served, with proofs of notice and service to all parties entitled to notice of an estate proceeding and all parties against whom relief is sought.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LENNIE L. GAINES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/17/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7, and 14.59 E.] Notwithstanding the lack of courtesy copies, the petition was skimmed online as a ONE-TIME courtesy. The petition asks that Louis L. Garret, Sr., be appointed as administrator, when the petitioner appears to spell his name Garrett. Petitioner and the deceased spouse are missing from item 8 of the petition. The date of death of any pre-deceased heir must be set forth. [LRSF 14.18 C.] The Waiver of Bond is incomplete at item 4. The Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete at item 2. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/6/22 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
